<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_dagger_ +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d4+9 piercing plus 1d6 evil

**Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 29, attack +21
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)
- **1st**
[Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) (3 slots)
- **2nd**
[Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [Touch of Idiocy](/Spells.aspx?ID=341) (3 slots)
- **3rd**
[Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180) (3 slots)
- **4th**
[Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [Resilient Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=255) (3 slots)
- **5th**
[Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Shadow Siphon](/Spells.aspx?ID=274) (3 slots)

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 29
- **4th**
[Blink](/Spells.aspx?ID=27), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Discern Lies](/Spells.aspx?ID=74), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)

**Rituals** DC 29

- **1st**
[Infernal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=23)

**Every Dagger** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The uniila wields four daggers; **Effect** The uniila attempts a single dagger Strike against a target. If this Strike hits, the uniila deals three extra dice of weapon damage.

**Fearsome Magic** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) **Frequency** Once per hour; **Effect** If the uniila's next action is to cast a harmful spell, creatures who fail their saving throw against the spell are [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 2 on a critical failure), in addition to the spell's other effects.

**Witch Token**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33)) (1 hour) **Frequency** Once per day; **Effect** The uniila creates a token in the shape of a piece of jewelry. When the _witch token_ is [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=98) by a mortal, all of the mortal's spells, except those cast at the highest level the mortal can cast, count as 1 level higher for the purpose of [counteracting](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) other spells and being counteracted. The creature can also Activate the _witch token_ to Cast a Spell once per day. This spell is chosen from one of the uniila's arcane spontaneous spells of level 4 or lower when the uniila creates the token. While a creature has the _witch token_ invested, it treats its saving throws against the uniila's spells as one degree of success worse, and the uniila always knows the distance and direction to the creature. A creature can't have more than one _witch token_ invested, and an uniila can't have more than one _witch token_ in existence. The uniila can destroy their _witch token_ as a free action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait; if the _witch token_ is invested when the uniila does so, the creature that has the witch token invested takes 9d8 mental damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Uniila Occultists</title>

Some uniilas study occult magic, rather than arcane magic, to better manipulate mystery cults or eldritch cabals. These more subtle uniilas cast occult spells instead of arcane spells; they cast [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) instead of [_glitterdust_](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) instead of [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [_modify memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) instead of [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), and [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) instead of [_ray of frost_](/Spells.aspx?ID=245).
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-30" />
name: Uniila

<title level="1" pfs="">[Hurlilu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2521)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

[Pazuzu's](/Deities.aspx?ID=79) [Abyssal](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) realm of High M'Vania is perched on the edge of an impossibly tall cliff. The demon lord and his minions claim the air around the cliff, guarding it against enemies and usurpers alike. Hurlilus, resembling horned, humanoid hawks with blood-red taloned feet and wings in place of arms, patrol these skies at their lord's command. Yet, hurlilus are surly and rebellious by nature, preferring to lead rather than follow orders. They'd prefer to spend their time bullying smaller creatures in High M'Vania's vast cliffside cities, but they're unwelcome there and driven out when discovered by occupants with greater authority (or by those who want to earn greater authority).

 Hurlilus make good guardians, particularly when they have leave to travel over great distances on patrol routes and can engage in spontaneous cruelty if the opportunity arises. However, they chafe in Pazuzu's service. Some forsake High M'Vania entirely and migrate to other parts of the Abyss, where they lead gangs of lesser fiends they've bullied into submission. A few hurlilus have made their way to Golarion, where they roost in aeries with subservient [harpies](/Monsters.aspx?ID=258), [manticores](/Monsters.aspx?ID=294), or other foul flying creatures. A dozen or so hurlilus serve high positions in the Mivanians, the brutal enforcers of the Halana Theocracy.

- **5th**
[Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=367)
- **6th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94) (at will), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143)

**Dive Bomb** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The hurlilu Strides using its fly Speed and makes a Strike at the end of that movement.

**Flesh Tearer**   If a hurlilu critically hits a foe with a melee Strike, the foe's flat check to recover from any resulting [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) damage is DC 19 instead of DC 15.

</column>

An oppali is a tenacious, twisting vine with white, trumpet-shaped flowers that grows in temperate and tropical regions. Stories passed down through generations refer to the oppali as a “bad luck vine” and warn against letting one take over a copse or garden, lest “your luck drain away like water down a hole.” This folklore contains more wisdom than most farmers or settlers realize. Oppalis are usually 30 feet long, but they twist and coil on themselves so much that they occupy a space not much larger than a horse. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Oppali](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2522) **Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 89 **Perception** +19; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), soundsense (precise) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +21 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +3 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -1 **Soundsense** An oppali's sense of hearing is a [precise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) to a range of 60 feet. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +21 **Will** +17 **HP** 175 **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **Energizing Incompetence** **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet critically fails an attack against the oppali; **Effect** The attack instead becomes a failure, and the oppali becomes [quickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 round. It can use this extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** vine +23 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 2d8+11 bludgeoning plus drain luck **Ranged** staccato blast +23 ([range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)), **Damage** 4d6 sonic **Drain Luck** ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A creature other than an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) hit by an oppali's vine Strike must attempt a DC 29 Fortitude saving throw twice, taking the lower result as the actual result.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Drain Luck for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature takes 2d6 poison damage. Until the start of the creature's next turn, the creature must roll twice and take the worse result on all checks.
**Failure** The creature takes 2d6 [persistent poison](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) damage. For 1 round, the creature must roll twice and take the worse result on all checks.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 4d6 persistent poison damage. For 1 minute, the creature must roll twice and take the worse result on all checks. **Trumpet Blast** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The oppali emits a massive blast of discordant noise that deals 9d8 sonic damage to creatures within a 60-foot cone (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). Creatures who fail the save are also [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 round. The oppali can't use its Trumpet Blast again for 1d4 rounds or until it takes at least 20 points of sonic damage from a single source (whichever comes first).
Oppali Trumpets An oppali's tough trumpets are useful for creating [thunderstones](/Equipment.aspx?ID=79). Incorporating crushed oppali trumpet into crafting the thunderstone grants a +2 item bonus to the [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check, provided that this material makes up at least half of the raw materials used for the thunderstone. With a successful DC 27 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) check, a character harvests 5d20 gp of this raw material from a defeated oppali (double on a critical success, half on a failure, and none at all from a critical failure).
name: Oppali

Ghalzarokh demons, also known as tyranny demons, form from the souls of cruel autocrats. These demons subjugate weaker fiends. After amassing an army, a ghalzarokh begins a chaotic spree of bloody conquest. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Ghalzarokh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2523) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 80 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet, [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +24 **Will** +26 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 330 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, good 15 **Defiance Vulnerability** Defiance causes a ghalzarokh anguish. If a creature scores a critical success on a saving throw to resist the ghalzarokh's [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), or [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) spells or Project Blame, the demon takes 4d6 mental damage. The demon takes the same damage if it rolls a critical failure on an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check.
**Project Blame** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Trigger** A creature succeeds or critically succeeds at a saving throw against one of the ghalzarokh's spells or Abyssal Spew, or the ghalzarokh misses a creature with a melee Strike; **Effect** The creature must attempt a DC 36 Will saving throw.
**Critical Success** The creature is temporarily immune to Project Blame for 24 hours, and the ghalzarokh suffers from its defiance vulnerability.
**Success** The creature's [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) condition increases by 1 (if they succeeded on a saving throw) or its [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) condition increases by 1 (if the demon missed them with a Strike). This increase in value lasts until the end of the creature's next turn but can't increase either condition beyond a value of 3.
**Failure** As success, but the duration changes to 1 minute. A creature can attempt a DC 36 Will save at the end of its turn to remove the effect early.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** jaws +30 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing plus 1d6 evil **Melee** claw +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Divine Innate Spells** DC 36 - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (at will) - **5th** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) (at will) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) - **8th** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **Constant (8th)** [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **Rituals** DC 36 - **1st** [Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21) **Abyssal Spew** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The ghalzarokh spews corrosive flame that deals 8d6 acid damage and 8d6 fire damage in a 40-foot cone (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The ghalzarokh can't use Abyssal Spew again for 1d4 rounds. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** claw
Demon Thralls Ghalzarokhs are bullies who prefer numerous thralls to allies. These demons loathe owing any other being a favor and serve stronger creatures only with the expectation of rich reward. A ghalzarokh can rarely be found without a fiendish entourage or army (such as a demon throng). They prefer “pacts” with “lesser” demons, usually offering only a feeble guarantee to allow the weaker fiend's continued existence.
name: Ghalzarokh

Born of the souls of doomsayers and cult leaders who sought to undermine and unravel reality, katpaskirs are demons of nihilism and unmaking. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Katpaskir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2524) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 81 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271), warp sense **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +31, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +31 **Str** +6 **Dex** +5 **Con** +9 **Int** +7 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 41 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +29 **Will** +30 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 415 **Immunities** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, good 15 **Breach Planar Wards** **Trigger** An effect (save only for those of items with the [artifact](/Traits.aspx?ID=253) trait) attempts to prevent the katpaskir from using a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effect or from casting [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=323); **Effect** The katpaskir attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering effect (counteract modifier of +29).
**Distortion Field** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 30 feet. Reality bends and warps on the level of all senses without displacing the katpaskir's actual location. Creatures of the katpaskir's choice who start their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or treat the area as greater difficult terrain and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=456) (DC 20). A creature who succeeds still treats the area as difficult terrain. For such creatures, the distance through the aura is doubled for determining range penalty.
**Mirrored Summons** **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet that the katpaskir is aware of uses magic to summon or otherwise conjure a good-aligned creature; **Effect** The katpaskir casts [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=323), regaining the daily ability to do so if needed. This effect is automatically sustained as long as the triggering summoning is sustained, for up to 1 minute.
**Warp Sense** The katpaskir senses changes in the planar fabric within 1 mile, including any [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effect, sensing the direction and distance to the disturbance. If it senses such a disturbance within 500 feet, the katpaskir can cast [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=239) to observe the area without needing line of sight to the location. --- **Speed** 35 feet, burrow 15 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** claw +34 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d12+14 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Melee** talon +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Divine Innate Spells** DC 40, attack +32 - **4th** [Dimensional Anchor](/Spells.aspx?ID=70) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Prying Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=239) (at will) - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) - **8th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=19) (×3 blink), [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76) - **9th** [Summon Fiend](/Spells.aspx?ID=323) (demons only) - **Constant (9th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **Rituals** DC 40 - **1st** [Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21) **Dimensional Ambush** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) The katpaskir casts [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), then makes a melee Strike that deals three extra dice of damage. This Strike counts as two attacks when calculating the katpaskir's multiple attack penalty.
Rift Makers Katpaskirs sense weaknesses in planar fabric. These demons worm their way into spaces between dimensions and break down the barriers. They leave holes aimed at causing chaos and dissolution. In this way, katpaskirs gnaw at reality, hoping to one day unravel it.
name: Katpaskir

The typical demon throng consists of a chaotic mix of less powerful demons— typically [abrikandilus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1110), [dretches](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1109), and [quasits](/Monsters.aspx?ID=97), with a few [brimoraks](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1111), [babaus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=598) or other, more powerful demons swept up in the mayhem. This tangled mess of different creatures makes coordinated tactics untenable but, while individual demons in the throng might not be able to fully utilize their abilities, the throng itself makes up for these deficiencies with dangerous options of its own. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Demonic Rabble](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2525) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 82 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +21 **Will** +21 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 260 (16 squares) **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10, good 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet, DC 30
**Serenity Vulnerability** A demonic rabble thrives on mayhem, and when members of the throng find themselves facing moments of calm, however brief or temporary, the throng suffers. Whenever the demonic rabble fails a saving throw against an affect that would normally [fascinate](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), [fatigue](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [paralyze](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrain](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), or [slow](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) at least four creatures, the troop takes 3d6 mental damage.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Claws, Fangs, and Horns** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The throng rips and tears at each enemy within its squares and within 5 feet (DC 33 Reflex save). Damage depends on the number of actions.
**Damage** 1d10+12 slashing plus 1d6 evil
**Damage** 2d10+12 slashing plus 1d6 evil
**Damage** 3d10+12 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Demonic Tide** A demonic rabble is less organized and more vicious than most troops. They can move into other creatures' spaces, and other creatures can move into their spaces. Their spaces are difficult terrain to non-demon creatures. The first time during a turn that a creature hostile to the throng willingly moves into their space, that creature is subjected to the one-action version of Claws, Fangs, and Horns. **[Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43)** **Hurl Debris** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The throng hurls debris, creating a 10-foot burst within 30 feet (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). When the throng is reduced to 8 or fewer squares, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. Damage depends on the number of actions.
**Damage** 2d10 bludgeoning
**Damage** 4d10 bludgeoning
**Damage** 6d10 bludgeoning **Troop Movement** Whenever the rabble Strides, they first [Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43) as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares), then moves up to their Speed.
name: Demonic Rabble

[Wrath demons](/Monsters.aspx?ID=100) are among the most well-known and feared of the Abyss's fiends, but sometimes a wrath demon becomes stunted in size as the result of too many failed rampages, a punishment inflicted by an angry demonic commander or simple ill fortune. These smaller wrath demons, not much larger than humans, are typically slaughtered quickly by their larger kin, but rarely a group of undersized vrocks can gather in numbers large enough to form a wrath riot. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Wrath Riot](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2526) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 83 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +27 **Will** +26 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 300 (16 squares) **Resistances** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 15 **Weaknesses** area damage 20, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, good 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 15 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet, DC 34
**Serenity Vulnerability** A wrath riot thrives on mayhem, and when members of the throng find themselves facing moments of calm, however brief or temporary, the throng suffers. Whenever the wrath riot fails a saving throw against an affect that would normally [fascinate](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), [fatigue](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [paralyze](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrain](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), or [slow](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) at least four creatures, the troop takes 3d6+10 mental damage
**Spores of Wrath** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) 5 feet. Non-[demons](/Traits.aspx?ID=42) that start their turn in the aura take 4d8 poison damage. Each creature damaged this way must succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save or take 2d8 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) as the spores penetrate its skin and grow into thick, green vines. The vines cease growing after 10 rounds, and they wither away in 1d4 days if not shaved off before then. The vines can be destroyed if the creature is affected by a [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) spell or if [_holy water_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=245) is applied to the vines (with an Interact action).
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet **Beaks, Claws, and Talons** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The throng furiously tears at each enemy within its squares and within 5 feet (DC 37 Reflex save). Damage depends on the number of actions.
**Damage** 1d12+14 slashing plus 1d6 evil
**Damage** 2d12+14 slashing plus 1d6 evil
**Damage** 3d12+14 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Demonic Tide** A wrath riot is less organized and more vicious than most troops. They can move into other creatures' spaces, and other creatures can move into their spaces. Their spaces are difficult terrain to non-[demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=42) creatures. The first time during a turn that a creature hostile to the throng willingly moves into their space, that creature is subjected to the one-action version of Beaks, Claws, and Talons. **Festival of Ruin** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Each non-[demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=42) creature in the throng or within a 20-foot emanation from it takes damage based on the number of actions used (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Damage depends on the number of actions.
**Damage** 4d6 electricity
**Damage** 8d6 electricity
**Damage** 12d6 electricity **[Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43)** **Troop Movement** Whenever the riot Strides, they first [Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43) as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares), then moves up to their Speed.
name: Wrath Riot

<title level="1" pfs="">[Derhii](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2527)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Legend suggests that Shory mages might have created derhiis as soldiers. Although folklore can be in error, little doubt exists that these flying apes make excellent warriors. History is also clear that derhiis fought countless battles for the Shory Empire, honing their tactical skills and martial traditions. Regardless of whether Shory magic was responsible for the birth of the first derhiis, lore passed down from those times shows the Shory Empire honored their soldiers and treated them as valued allies rather than mere servants.

 Derhiis are scarce even in the Mwangi Expanse, where the last of the Shory flying cities, such as Kho and Ulduvai, fell. Still, some dwell among the spires that tower above Shory ruins; these derhii are often quite a bit more powerful than the one detailed below. Other derhiis have spread from the remains of the fallen Shory empire to populate remote highlands and old-growth forests.

 Derhiis form small filial groups called troops, centered on one elder derhii and their relations. Several troops might gather into a larger society with a council of troop leaders under one dominant elder.

 Derhiis are omnivores who favor meat, unlike the terrestrial and largely herbivorous gorillas they resemble. It's possible that this habit developed due to a derhii need for substantial amounts of food coupled with their soldierly ways.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Derhii.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Derhii](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2527)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 84

**Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +2

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Falchion](/Weapons.aspx?ID=18), [Javelin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=71) (6), [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 80

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet, fly 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
falchion +14 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)),
**Damage** 1d10+7 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
javelin +14 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 piercing

**Booming Voice**  ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A derhii can use its voice as a signal. The sound can be heard up to 12 miles away as a thrumming in the air that conveys 20 words of information over the course of 5 minutes.

**Critical Knockdown**   When a derhii scores a critical hit with a melee Strike, the target also falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

**Power Dive** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The derhii Strides twice while flying, then makes a Strike with a manufactured weapon. If the derhii moved at least 20 feet from their starting position, the Strike's damage is increases by one die, such as increasing falchion damage to 2d10+7. With a melee attack, the derhii can forgo the extra damage to force the target to fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Derhii Artistry</title>

Derhiis have little use for money, but they're talented crafters. Their goods are practical, beautiful and, when needed, deadly. Shory influences can still be seen in derhii art, including crystalline designs, beadwork, and geometric patterns that hint at the arcane. Derhii leatherwork and metalwork is also top notch, owing to their desire for functional, durable armaments.
</aside>
name: Derhii

[Daemons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=26), including [leukodaemons](/Monsters.aspx?ID=90), once ruled over part of the fallen city of Kho. While they did, they created the first hadis. Hadis look more fiendish and sinister than typical [ratfolk](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=20). In hadi society, those who foster and cultivate blights and sickness are alchemists known as “tafens”. Others, known as “rajwans,” focus their training on brutal combat and barbarian rages. Individually, most hadis are no match for a high level group, but when confronted in their lair, they can gather together into a dangerous mob. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Hadi Mob](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2528) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 85 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); ratspeak **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29 **Str** +6 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Ratspeak** A hadi mob can communicate with rodents. --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +29 **Will** +23 **HP** 270 (16 squares) **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 10 feet **Gnaw and Chew** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** the hadi mob bites and tears into the flesh of each enemy adjacent to the troop (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
2d8+8 slashing damage plus hadi pestilence
4d8+12 slashing damage plus hadi pestilence
6d8+12 slashing damage plus hadi pestilence **Hadi Pestilence** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) This daemonic disease turns the victim into a hadi. **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier (1 day); **Stage 2** [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (1 day); **Stage 3** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 day); **Stage 4** drained 2 (1 day); **Stage 5** drained 3 (1 week); **Stage 6** transform into a hadi (1 year). For a PC, the best way to model this is to replace ancestry Hit Points, size, Speeds, ability boosts, ability flaws, traits, and special abilities with those of the [ratfolk](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=20) ancestry. The character loses previous ancestry feats, selecting replacements from the [ratfolk ancestry](/Feats.aspx?Traits=234). **[Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43)** **Troop Movement** Whenever the hadi mob Strides, they first [Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43) as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares), then moves up to their Speed. This works just like a Gargantuan creature moving; for instance, if any square of the troop enters difficult terrain, the extra movement cost applies to the whole troop.
Hadi Evil Although the original hadis created by [daemons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=26) in ages past were ascended from rats infused with fiendish qualities that tempered their sapience with an engineered evil, over the generations the hadi people have formed their own society. They cling to evil ways largely out of tradition or fear, but there are an increasing number of hadis who admire Kho's [derhiis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2527) and seek their own paths. Others, particularly those who have traveled far from Kho, have come to understand the plight of their folk under the weight of a fiendish past. Non-evil hadi thus exist, but they are viewed by those in positions of power in Kho as dangers to the social order. These hadi do not carry the pestilence their more sinister kin do and many actively work to suppress the spread of illnesses.
name: Hadi Mob

Ephialtes velstracs are consummate and fearless hunters of the doomed and the damned. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Ephialtes](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2529) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 86 **Perception** +30; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), painsight, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +28, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +29 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 **Painsight** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) conditions, as well as the value of those conditions. --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +25 **Will** +28 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 299 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by good or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Weaknesses** good 15, silver 15 **Nowhere to Run** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it feels a powerful sensation of hopelessness. The creature must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (slowed 2 on a critical failure) for 1 round. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** jaws +33 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+13 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Melee** chain +33 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 3d6+13 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 37 - **6th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Dimensional Anchor](/Spells.aspx?ID=70) (at will) - **7th** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=28), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self only), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=287) (x3) - **8th** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36), [Discern Location](/Spells.aspx?ID=75), [Shadow Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=275) - **Constant (8th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d4+13 slashing, DC 37 **Exhale Chains** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) The ephialtes exhales a tangle of barbed chains. All creatures in a 50-foot cone must attempt a DC 37 Reflex save. The ephialtes can't Exhale Chains for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** It takes 8d6 piercing damage.
**Failure** It takes 15d6 piercing damage, 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but 30d6 piercing damage, 2d6 persistent bleed damage, and is [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) until they escape (DC 37). **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68)) The ephialtes stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must attempt a Will save against the nowhere to run aura. If it was already [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), on a failed save its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn. After attempting this save, the targeted creature is then temporarily immune to Focus Gaze until the start of the ephialtes's next turn.
Ephialtes Prey When they catch enough quarry, an ephialtes velstrac returns to the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11), bringing victims for other velstracs to use for training or raw material. Velstracs respect ephialtes for their “generosity” in guiding and providing for others of their kind, yet are always wary about making assumptions about the limits of this generosity.
name: Ephialtes

Prison keepers among velstracs, vincuvicar velstracs thrive on depriving others of freedom. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Vincuvicar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2530) **Source** [Pathfinder #191: The Destiny War](/Sources.aspx?ID=199) pg. 87 **Perception** +32; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), painsight, [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +29, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +34, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +32, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +30, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +31 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [war flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=46)_, _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_, [Manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32) (6) --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +28 **Will** +30 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 333 , regeneration 25 (deactivated by good or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) **Weaknesses** good 20, silver 20 **Alone and Forgotten** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it feels imprisoned and must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or become [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Institutionalized** A vincuvicar forms a bond with an institution that holds prisoners. This takes 1 day and ends any previous bond the vincuvicar had. While within the area of the prison, the vincuvicar gains a +2 status bonus to skill checks and Perception checks. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** war flail +35 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 3d10+17 bludgeoning plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** animated manacles +32 ([range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+8 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 40 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **8th** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Mind Blank](/Spells.aspx?ID=192), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=193), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) - **9th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (lawful), [Prying Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=239) - **Constant (9th)** [Discern Lies](/Spells.aspx?ID=74), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Divine Rituals** DC 40 - **8th** [Imprisonment](/Rituals.aspx?ID=13) **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The vincuvicar stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must attempt a Will save against the alone and forgotten aura. If the creature was already [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35), on a failed save, its hopelessness causes it to be enfeebled 2 and unable to take the [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) action as long as they remain enfeebled. After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune until the start of the vincuvicar's next turn. **Shackle** **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet is damaged by the vincuvicar's animated manacles Strike; **Effect** The creature must attempt a DC 40 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature avoids being manacled.
**Success** The manacles distract the creature, causing it to become [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of its next turn.
**Failure** The manacles fuse in place and [bind the creature's legs and arms](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 40) or the manacles are destroyed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33).
Vincuvicar Thrones Each vincuvicar possesses a personal, spiked iron throne—a symbol of authority. This iron throne is an object with Hardness 20 and 500 Hit Points. If a vincuvicar's throne is destroyed, the velstrac must return to the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) to acquire or construct a replacement.
name: Vincuvicar

markdown:
A sand wolf is an elemental creature which dwells in the sandy deserts of elemental earth that drift to, or have been conjured on, the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=4). Occasionally, these creatures seep through planar rifts or are summoned by elementalists on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). Sand wolves are prized as guardians for their relative intelligence, keen perception, and ability to disguise themselves, easily blending with the shifting sands in desert terrain. Cunning and agile, they fiercely pursue any creatures they ambush, no matter how nimble their prey. Sand wolves also have a remarkable ability to sing beautiful, haunting songs, and their music is considered high entertainment among djinn who inhabit the Plane of Air. Many [djinni](/Monsters.aspx?ID=212) viziers and even certain Material Plane rulers seeking the attention of their peers keep at least one of these creatures in their employ to show off at special events and ceremonies. The wolves' wordless songs evoke both the strength of the earth and the power of wind to erode it. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Sand Wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2531) **Source** [The Enmity Cycle](/Sources.aspx?ID=200) pg. 62 **Perception** +14; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +15 **Will** +9 **HP** 92 **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) 5, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 5, piercing 5, slashing 5 **Water Vulnerability** Sand wolves take 5 additional damage from attacks and effects with the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait.
**Collapse Into Sand** **Trigger** The sand wolf is hit by an attack; **Effect** The sand wolf's body falls apart into a cloud of sand. It takes no damage from the triggering attack, and its resistances increase to 8. The sand wolf must spend one action to reform its body before it can move or attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 20 feet, fly 20 feet **Melee** jaws +14, **Damage** 2d6+5 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Ranged** sand blast +12 ([range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10 slashing **Choking Sands** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The sand wolf discorporates into a cloud of whirling sand. It can Stride, moving freely through occupied spaces. Any creatures within those spaces take 2d10 damage and must attempt a DC 19 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The target takes no damage.
**Success** The target takes half damage.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect is increased to sickened 3. **Susurrating Song** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The sand wolf sings a short, enchanting song. Any creature other than a sand wolf within 30 feet must attempt a DC 19 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to further susurrating song exposures for 24 hours.
**Success** The target is stupefied 1.
**Failure** The target is stupefied 2.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the target is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).
The Eternal Hourglass Deep within a cavern in the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5) is a massive, seemingly indestructible hourglass the size of a small city, its top filled with thousands of sand wolves. As each wolf reaches the opening to the bottom, they are turned to sand and do not reform, instead piling up in a huge mound. It is not clear who constructed or placed the hourglass, how the sand wolves got there, or what—if anything—the hourglass is timing. Some claim the wolves will break loose and rampage freely when the sand runs out, while others believe the glass is counting down to the end of time itself.
name: Sand Wolf

markdown:
In places where magic, alchemy, and engineering converge, there are often unforeseen side effects on the environment and its inhabitants. Scrapborn sometimes result from this strange convergence. Mostly, scrapborn appear to be walking piles of assorted junk, seemingly assembled at random. Their actual bodies are vaguely humanoid with delicate, spongy green skin. They secrete an adhesive that allows them to gather up debris and attach it to their bodies for protection. When these scraps eventually fall off, the scrapborn either reattaches them or replaces them with something new. Despite their resulting bulky appearance, scrapborn remain strong swimmers and display little loss of agility underwater. Scrapborn lack a centralized society, but sometimes congregate in small groups that work together for survival. Typically, this leads to ever-escalating attacks on nearby settlements as the creatures feed upon each other's resentment and work themselves up into a frenzy. They often target places with high-quality armor first so they can improve their odds of survival. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Scrapborn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2532) **Source** [The Enmity Cycle](/Sources.aspx?ID=200) pg. 63 **Perception** +10; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +11 **Will** +9 **HP** 81 **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) 5 **Sacrifice Armor** **Trigger** The scrapborn is damaged by an attack dealing physical damage; **Effect** The scrapborn directs the damage to one of the pieces of scrap attached to its body and gains resistance 15 against the triggering attack, but its AC is lowered by 2 for 24 hours. The scrapborn's AC cannot be reduced below 16 in this way. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** claw +14, **Damage** 2d8+5 piercing **Ranged** thrown scrap +13, **Damage** 2d8+2 bludgeoning **Rebuild Armor** **Requirements** The scrapborn's previous action was a successful claw Strike against a target wearing armor and that armor is not [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2); **Effect** The scrapborn pulls and rips at the target's armor. The target must attempt a DC 20 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target's armor takes 1d8 piercing damage, ignoring the armor's Hardness.
**Failure** The target's armor takes 2d8 piercing damage, which ignores the armor's Hardness; if this causes the armor to become broken, the scrapborn can incorporate the removed armor piece into its own body and raise its AC by 1 (to a maximum of 22).
**Critical Failure** As failure, except instead of damage, the target's armor is automatically reduced to its BT and the scrapborn increases its AC by 2.
Pretend Knights Some scrapborn spend a great deal of their time carefully selecting the pieces of armor and other bits they attach to their sticky skin. They might enjoy a particular color or material and cultivate their collection accordingly, striving for a cohesive design over time. A very diligent and patient—or particularly wealthy— scrapborn can even construct a full suit of armor this way and might even be able to pass as a knight, using their bulky armored form to bluff and intimidate anyone who might question them as they walk through a city or castle.
name: Scrapborn

**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Zridi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2542) **Source** [The Enmity Cycle](/Sources.aspx?ID=200) pg. 49 **Perception** +16; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Osiriani](/Languages.aspx?ID=40); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +14, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +7 **Items** [_horn of fog_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=258) --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +17 **Will** +18 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 125 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 6, good 6 **Hatred of Red** Pairakas hate the color red. Zridi won't wear the color or willingly enter any place painted red. Given a choice, she'll attack a creature wearing red first. If barred from expressing her displeasure toward the color by force or some magical effect, she takes 2d6 mental damage at the end of her turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet **Melee** claw +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing plus 1d6 evil and bubonic plague **Divine Innate Spells** DC 26 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (at will), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Misdirection](/Spells.aspx?ID=198) (at will; self only), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (at will) **Rituals** DC 26 - **1st** [Div Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=46) **Bubonic Plague** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) A creature can't remove the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition while infected; **Saving Throw** DC 24 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 day; **Stage 1** fatigued (1 day); **Stage 2** [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and fatigued (1 day); **Stage 3** enfeebled 3, fatigued, and take 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) every 1d20 minutes (1 day) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Zridi can take the appearance of any Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) or [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9). This doesn't change her Speed or her attack and damage modifiers with the Strikes, but it might change the damage type her Strikes deal. **Compelled Performance** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Zridi grants the target a +2 status bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks for one week. The target must attempt a DC 26 Will save.
**Critical Success** No further effect.
**Success** The target must [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 action on its next turn.
**Failure** The target must Perform for 3 actions on its next turn, undertaking a Performance task set by Zridi.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but Zridi can choose to delay the effect and trigger it at any point in the next week as a reaction. **Tormenting Dreams** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Zridi torments a sleeping creature within 100 feet with visions of betrayals by loved ones and friends. The target must attempt a DC 26 Will save, with the effects of the [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) spell.
name: Zridi

This Strike doesn't contribute to his multiple attack penalty, and his multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** _rapier_ +22 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+11 slashing **Melee** beak +21 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d4+11 piercing **Ranged** _spike launcher_ +22 ([Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172), [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [Kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d8+9 piercing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 26 - **4th** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94) **Go for the Eyes** **Trigger** Raskus critically hits with a beak Strike; **Effect** Raskus attempts to peck out his victim's eyes. The target must attempt a DC 29 Fortitude save.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is blinded until healed to its maximum Hit Points. **Opportunistic Attack** Raskus's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 damage to any foe that has a reduction to its Speed, is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1), or is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). **Quick Spiker** Raskus requires only 1 action, rather than 2 actions, to reload a [_spike launcher_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1171). He can change his grip on a spike launcher easily, improving its [fatal aim d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=404) trait to [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178). (These changes are incorporated into its ranged Strike, above.
name: Raskus

markdown:
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Kannijo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2548) **Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 39 **Perception** +23; breathsense (precise) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Breathsense** 60 feet ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)). A jiang-shi can't perceive living creatures beyond 5 feet except with their breathsense. A living creature within the listed range who holds its breath is invisible to the jiang-shi for as long as it hold its breath. To hold its breath in this way, a creature must have one hand free to fully plug its nose or cover its mouth. (Refer to [Drowning and Suffocation](/Rules.aspx?ID=537) for the full rules on holding breath.) **Items** [_The Snakebite_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2489) --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +25 **Will** +23 **HP** 180 ([fast healing 10](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15), [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), one more breath) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [darkwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=376)) **Jiang-Shi Vulnerabilities** All jiang-shi possess the following vulnerabilities.
  • **Revulsion** A jiang-shi can't voluntarily come within 10 feet of a brandished mirror or the sound of ringing handbells. A creature can Interact with a mirror or handbell to brandish or ring that item for 1 round (similar to [Raising a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98)). If the jiang-shi involuntarily comes within 10 feet of an object of their revulsion, they gain the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) condition, running from the object of their revulsion until they are 10 feet or farther from the object. After 1 round of being exposed to the object of their revulsion, a jiang-shi can attempt a DC 25 Will save as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. On a success, they overcome their revulsions for 1d6 rounds (or 1 hour on a critical success).
  • **Bitter Epiphany** A jiang-shi is afflicted by severe melancholia when reminded of their debased nature and the truth of their existential disconnection from living beings. When a jiang-shi sees an altar with at least 1 Bulk of food offerings to ancestor spirits, they must attempt a DC 25 Will save. They are then temporarily immune to bitter epiphany for 1 minute.
    **Critical Success** The jiang-shi is unaffected.
    **Success** The jiang-shi is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
    **Failure** The jiang-shi is stupefied 2 for 1 minute.
    **Critical Failure** The jiang-shi is stupefied 2 and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 minute.

**One More Breath** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) Unlike other undead, a jiang-shi isn't destroyed at 0 HP. Instead, they fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) and awaken in 1 minute with 1 Hit Point. Scattering at least 1 Bulk of glutinous rice or hen eggs on an unconscious jiang-shi destroys them permanently. If the jiang-shi was reduced to 0 HP by an attack from a weapon made of [darkwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=273), they're destroyed immediately
**Harrowed Fulu** Unlike the typical jiang-shi, Kannijo's warped fulu has been replaced by a card from the _Deck of Destiny_—a replacement that has largely gone unnoticed by the vampire. Kannijo is immune to spells cast from a magic item without expending a spell slot, such as from a [scroll](/Equipment.aspx?ID=640) or [wand](/Traits.aspx?ID=164), and also gains the passive abilities granted from _[The Snakebite](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2489)_ (but not its activation ability). A creature can [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) _The Snakebite_ to remove it (DC 33). This immediately ends Kannijo's immunity to these effects, negates the card's passive effects, and removes Kannijo's [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15). Kannijo can no longer use _The Snakebite_ as its fulu, but can create a replacement warped fulu by spending 1 uninterrupted hour inscribing a strip of paper (or similar) with a writing instrument. --- **Speed** 15 feet, swim 15 feet; rigor mortis **Melee** fangs +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d10+10 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) and spirit naga venom **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=286) - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=28), [Humanoid Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=153), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=39), [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=90), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) (4 slots) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=40), [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) (4 slots) - **5th** [Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=314), [Synesthesia](/Spells.aspx?ID=328) (4 slots) - **6th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=218), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=301) (4 slots) - **7th** [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) (3 slots) **Rituals** DC 33 - **2nd** [Inveigle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=14) **Drain Qi** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within Kannijo's reach; **Effect** The jiang-shi drains the victim's life energy, or qi, through their breath. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and the jiang-shi regains HP equal to 10% of its maximum HP, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points. Draining qi from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to the jiang-shi but increases the victim's drained condition value by 1. If the victim has the ability to cast ki spells, it can spend 1 Focus Point to avoid becoming drained. This protects it only from the current attack, not from subsequent attempts to Drain Qi.
A victim's drained condition value decreases by 1 every week. If the creature restricts their diet to glutinous rice for at least 1 day and spends 10 minutes dancing, jogging, or otherwise engaging in vigorous physical activity by succeeding at a DC 25 Athletics check, it reduces its drained condition by 1. **Manipulate the Green** Kannijo can use [_inveigle_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=14) against [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creatures normally immune to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects, and can communicate with inveigled plant creatures as if under the effects of [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294). **Rigor Mortis** The jiang-shi ignores difficult terrain and effects that would render it [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). A jiang-shi can't take the [Drop Prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=78) action. When a jiang-shi [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), it doesn't trigger reactions that are normally triggered by [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions, such as [Attacks of Opportunity](/Actions.aspx?ID=8). **Spirit Naga Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 33 (34 against [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) targets); **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d6 poison damage and stupefied 2 (1 round)

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Harpy Warbird](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2552) **Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 53 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +24 **Str** +5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=26)_, _+1 [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_ (20 arrows) --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +22 **Will** +20 **HP** 200 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** _halberd_ +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing **Melee** talon +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d4+11 slashing **Ranged** _shortbow_ +23 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+11 piercing **Captivating Song** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The harpy cries out an eerie, compelling melody. Each non-harpy creature within a 300-foot aura must attempt a DC 29 Will save to avoid becoming captivated by the harpy's song. The effect lasts for 1 round, but if the harpy uses this ability again on subsequent rounds, it extends the duration by 1 round for all affected creatures. Once a creature succeeds at any save against Captivating Song, that creature is temporarily immune to Captivating Songs for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), and it must spend each of its actions to move closer to the harpy as expediently as possible, while avoiding obvious dangers. If a captivated creature is adjacent to the harpy, it stays still and doesn't act. If attacked by the harpy, the creature is freed from captivation at the end of the harpy's turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but if attacked by the harpy, the creature can attempt a new save at the start of its next turn, rather than being freed at the end of the harpy's turn. **War Formation** A harpy warbird deals an extra 2d6 damage with its melee Strikes to any creature that's within reach of at least one of the harpy's allies.
name: Harpy Warbird

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Blade Magus](/NPCs.aspx?ID=2554) **Source** [Pathfinder #190: The Choosing](/Sources.aspx?ID=198) pg. 60 **Perception** +18 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Thassilonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=52) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +20 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Items** steel-colored [_bird feather token_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=244), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=26)_, [_moderate healing potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=186), _+1 [scale mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=8)_, [spellbook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=53) (contains all prepared spells) --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +17 **Will** +20 **HP** 195 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Capture Magic** **Trigger** The Blade magi succeeds at a save against a foe's harmful spell, or a foe fails a spell attack roll against the Blade magi with a harmful spell; **Effect** The Blade magi enters their Arcane Cascade. If the Blade magi is already in Arcane Cascade, they gain a +2 status bonus to damage from Arcane Cascade until the end of their next turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _halberd_ +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 30, attack +22 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Acid Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=3), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) - **5th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) - **6th** [Baleful Polymorph](/Spells.aspx?ID=17), [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33) **Magus Focus Spells** DC 30, 1 Focus Point - **6th** [Thunderous Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1044) **Arcane Cascade** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=152)) **Requirement** The Blade magi can enter this stance if their most recent action was to Cast a Spell or make a Spellstrike, but the Blade magi can remain in the stance even if they no longer meet the requirements; **Effect** When the Blade magi enters this stance and at the beginning of each of their turns while in it, the Blade magi gains 5 temporary Hit Points. While in this stance, the Blade magi's melee Strikes gain the [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11) trait, deal an extra 2 force damage, and deal 4 splash force damage to foes adjacent to the target. **Spellstrike** The Blade magi channels a spell into a melee Strike to deliver a magically charged attack. The Blade magi casts [_acid splash_](/Spells.aspx?ID=3), [_ray of frost_](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), or [_vampiric touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=354). The effects of the spell don't occur immediately but are imbued into the Blade magi's attack instead. The Blade magi makes a melee Strike, which gains the [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11) trait. The spell is coupled with the attack, using the Blade magi's attack roll to determine the effects of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks for their multiple attack penalty, but the penalty isn't applied until after the magus has completed the Spellstrike. After using Spellstrike, the Blade magi can't do so again until they recharge this ability as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. The Blade magi also recharges their Spellstrike when they cast [_thunderous strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1044).
name: Blade Magus
